eligibilityFreeScreen.description: This is the Standard-Version of the Load2day Driver Connect app. The Enterprise version includes the ability to verify your eligibility at participating terminals. For Information on how you can upgrade to the full version please click here
eligibilityFreeScreen.subtitle: Participating Terminals

eligibilityPaidScreen.subtitle: Participating terminals
eligibilityPaidScreen.descriptionLoadRackEligibility: This feature checks data validity for the selections made (expired fields and locked records) for a load
eligibilityPaidScreen.descriptionOrderElegibility: This feature checks data validity for the selections made (expired fields and locked records) for a specific order

signup.mailMessage: Your email will be used to log in to your account

signup.validationUpperWrong: X At least (1) one upper case
signup.validationSymbolWrong: X At least (1) one  special character

activationScreen.longTextHeader: Your account needs to be activated, please check your email to activate your account. If you haven't recieved an activation email please check the junk folder of your mailbox. If you have already activated your account tap the refresh button.

demoScreen.title: What’s In This Stack?
demoScreen.tagLine: Congratulations, you’ve got a very advanced React Native app template here.  Take advantage of this boilerplate!
demoScreen.androidReactotronHint: If this doesn't work, ensure the Reactotron desktop app is running, run adb reverse tcp:9090 tcp:9090 from your terminal, and reload the app.
demoScreen.iosReactotronHint: If this doesn't work, ensure the Reactotron desktop app is running and reload app.
demoScreen.macosReactotronHint: If this doesn't work, ensure the Reactotron desktop app is running and reload app.
demoScreen.webReactotronHint: If this doesn't work, ensure the Reactotron desktop app is running and reload app.
demoScreen.windowsReactotronHint: If this doesn't work, ensure the Reactotron desktop app is running and reload app.

errorScreen.title: Something went wrong!
errorScreen.friendlySubtitle: This is the screen that your users will see in production when an error is thrown. You'll want to customize this message (located in `app/i18n/en.json`) and probably the layout as well (`app/screens/error`). If you want to remove this entirely, check `app/app.tsx` for the <ErrorBoundary> component.
